title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array of n integers and a window size k, find the maximum value in each contiguous window of size k as it slides from left to right.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and k (1 <= k <= n <= 10^6). The second line contains n integers a_1, ..., a_n (-10^9 <= a_i <= 10^9).
output_spec: Print n - k + 1 integers, the maximum in each window.

routes.name: Monotone deque
routes.description: Maintain a deque of indices where elements are in decreasing order. The front always holds the current window maximum.

observations.title: Deque stores indices, not values
observations.description: Store indices in the deque so you can check whether the front element is still within the window.

hints.nudge: You need to know when an element leaves the window. What should you store?
hints.question: If you store values, how would you know when the maximum has left the window? What if you stored something else?
hints.formal: Store indices in the deque. To check if front is in window: front index >= i - k + 1. Access value via a[deque.front()].

observations.title: Maintain decreasing order from front to back
observations.description: Before adding a new element, pop all elements from the back that are smaller. This keeps the deque monotonically decreasing.

hints.nudge: What invariant should the deque maintain?
hints.question: When you add element a[i] to the deque, which existing elements can never be the answer for any future window?
hints.formal: While deque is non-empty and a[deque.back()] <= a[i]: pop back. Then push i. Elements smaller than a[i] will never be a future window max.

observations.title: Pop expired front
observations.description: Before reading the window maximum, check if the front index has left the window. If so, pop it.

hints.nudge: The front of the deque is the maximum, but is it still in the current window?
hints.question: If the window starts at index i-k+1 and deque.front() is an older index, what should you do?
hints.formal: If deque.front() < i - k + 1, pop from front. The new front is the current window maximum.

observations.title: O(n) amortized complexity
observations.description: Each element is pushed and popped at most once, giving O(n) total time despite the inner while loop.

hints.nudge: Is the while loop inside the for loop really O(n^2)?
hints.question: How many times total can an element be pushed and popped from the deque across all iterations?
hints.formal: Each of the n elements enters and leaves the deque at most once. Total operations = O(n). Per-window max is read from front in O(1).
